old_claim.note: Animal Husbandry is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim.note: The Wadi Jilat claim rests on managed domestic caprine herds whose isotope signatures and associated lithic traditions identify mobile herding communities.

source_shape.source_locator: Accepted manuscript pp. 22-23, Conclusion: combines caprine isotope and chipped-stone evidence to identify indigenous mobile communities adopting herds.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Wadi Jilat pastoralist interpretation depends on direct evidence for managed caprine herds moving within mobile steppe lifeways.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Herd management is not merely adjacent context here; without managed domestic animals, the scoped subsistence strategy would not be pastoralism.

ontology_before: Animal husbandry was represented as optional background to a broad pastoralism label.
ontology_after: Managed domestic herds are constitutive of the directly studied Wadi Jilat nomadic-pastoralist system.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pastoralism now requires animal_husbandry with primary evidence.
would_reject_if: The Wadi Jilat caprines were reclassified as unmanaged wild animals rather than domestic herds.
short_reason: The scoped pastoralism claim is defined and evidenced through managed herds.
